description_long: This is a pair of end-cutting pliers, also known as nippers, featuring two long, straight handles that converge at a rounded pivot joint. The jaws are semicircular and robust, designed for cutting through wire, nails, or other tough materials, with a flat cutting edge that is perpendicular to the handles. The tool is made primarily of metal, likely steel, with a slightly worn, matte finish that suggests frequent use. There are no visible rubber or plastic grips on the handles, indicating a utilitarian, industrial design. The pivot is a single, prominent rivet, allowing the jaws to open and close smoothly. A marking is visible on one handle, reading 'KNIPEX 180', which identifies the brand and model.
description: A robust pair of end-cutting pliers with long, straight metal handles and semicircular cutting jaws, finished in a worn matte metal. Marked with 'KNIPEX 180' on one handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing both straight handles and the rounded, flat cutting jaws with the 'KNIPEX 180' marking visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the handles and the thickness of the jaw assembly at the pivot.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back of the handles and the rounded jaw ends without visible markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the jaw's thickness, with some wear visible on the metal.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, uninsulated metal handles with a simple cylindrical profile.
attribute_jaw_design: Semicircular, end-cutting jaws with a flat cutting edge perpendicular to the handles, designed for nipping or cutting wire.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed rivet pivot, allowing standard opening and closing motion without adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible grip pattern or ergonomic features.
